content: Leukemias are hematologic malignancies characterized by abnormal proliferation of blood cells. Acute leukemias (rapid progression, immature blasts): Acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) - lymphoid origin, most common childhood cancer, also occurs in adults; Acute myeloid leukemia (AML) - myeloid origin, most common acute leukemia in adults, median age 68. Chronic leukemias (indolent, mature cells): Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) - mature B-cells, most common adult leukemia in Western countries; Chronic myeloid leukemia (CML) - myeloid lineage, Philadelphia chromosome (BCR-ABL fusion). AML classification (WHO 2022): by genetics (NPM1, CEBPA, RUNX1-RUNX1T1, CBFB-MYH11, PML-RARA), myelodysplasia-related, therapy-related, NOS. ALL: B-ALL (CD19+, CD10+, TdT+) vs T-ALL (cytoplasmic CD3+, CD7+). CLL: CD5+, CD23+, dim surface immunoglobulin, mutated vs unmutated IGHV status prognostic.
